meta.title: TB-CDA-2: Data Modeling, Calculations, and Dashboard Analysis

questions.stem: You need the fastest way to reduce rows returned from a database before Tableau renders the view. What should you use?
questions.explanation: **B** is correct because a data source filter can be pushed to the underlying database early, reducing transferred rows. **A**, **C**, and **D** do not reduce source rows before retrieval. [Optimize Workbook Performance - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/performance_tips.htm)

questions.stem: When should a Tableau extract usually be preferred over a live connection?

options.text: When you need offline use or faster repeated analysis on stable data
When the database must always answer every interaction in real time
When you want row-level security disabled
When you need more dimensions than Tableau supports

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because extracts often improve repeated analysis performance and support offline scenarios. **B** favors live connections, while **C** and **D** are incorrect. [Extract Your Data - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/extracting_data.htm)

questions.stem: What is the key modeling difference between Tableau relationships and physical joins?

options.text: Relationships keep tables separate until query time at the logical layer
Relationships always duplicate every row immediately
Joins are only available for extracts

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because relationships operate at the logical layer and preserve separate tables until analysis time. **B**, **C**, and **D** are incorrect. [The Tableau Data Model - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/datasource_datamodel.htm)

questions.stem: Which TWO tasks are common reasons to use a physical join instead of only logical relationships? (Select TWO)

options.text: You must flatten rows before analysis
You need all fields in one denormalized row-level table
You want Tableau to decide table combination at query time automatically
You want to avoid any effect from join cardinality

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because some workflows require explicit row-level flattening. **B** is correct because a physical join creates a combined row structure. **C** describes relationships, and **D** is false because join cardinality still matters. [The Tableau Data Model - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/datasource_datamodel.htm)

questions.stem: A text field stores values like `$1,245.75`. What is the safest Tableau approach for calculations?

options.text: Convert it with a calculated field that strips symbols then casts to a number
Sort it alphabetically and sum the strings
Place it on Color and let Tableau infer currency
Use a dashboard action to change the data type

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because you typically remove symbols and separators before casting the string to a numeric type. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not produce reliable numeric calculations. [Data Types - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/datafields_typesandroles_datatypes.htm)

questions.stem: Which Tableau feature groups related dimension members into a single higher-level member without changing source data?
questions.explanation: **B** is correct because groups combine members into named categories. **A** creates numeric buckets, **C** controls animation/iteration, and **D** is too specific and not the same concept. [Create Sets - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/sortgroup_sets_create.htm)

questions.stem: What is the primary use of bins in Tableau?

options.text: To create fixed-size buckets for continuous numeric values

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because bins segment continuous numeric values into ranges for histograms and similar views. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. [Create Bins from a Continuous Measure - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_bins.htm)

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about data types in Tableau are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: Changing a field to date enables date-aware functions and hierarchies
String values with currency symbols may need cleaning before numeric conversion
Data types only matter after publishing to Server
A geographic role is identical to a data type

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because date typing affects Tableau date behavior. **B** is correct because dirty currency strings often require cleanup. **C** is false, and **D** confuses geographic roles with data types. [Data Types - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/datafields_typesandroles_datatypes.htm)

questions.stem: An analyst needs all orders preserved, with returns attached when present and NULL otherwise. Which join is most appropriate if Orders is the left table?
questions.explanation: **B** is correct because a left join preserves all rows from the left table and matches returns where available. **A** would drop non-returned orders, **C** preserves the opposite side, and **D** stacks rows instead of matching them. [The Tableau Data Model - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/datasource_datamodel.htm)

questions.stem: Why might a `FIXED` level-of-detail expression ignore a regular dimension filter?

options.text: Because FIXED LODs are computed before dimension filters unless the filter is in context
Because FIXED LODs only work on extracts
Because dimension filters cannot affect any calculations
Because Tableau disables filters on dashboards

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Tableau order of operations evaluates FIXED LOD expressions before regular dimension filters unless those filters become context filters. **B**, **C**, and **D** are incorrect. [Level of Detail Expressions - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_calculatedfields_lod.htm)

questions.stem: Which expression type computes an aggregate at a specified level independent of the view’s current level of detail?
questions.explanation: **B** is correct because LOD expressions explicitly define calculation granularity. **A** is computed after the query result set, and **C** and **D** are not calculation types. [Level of Detail Expressions - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_calculatedfields_lod.htm)

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of a parameter in Tableau?

options.text: To provide a user-controlled value that calculations, filters, or reference lines can use

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because parameters expose a selectable value that other workbook logic can reference. **A**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. [Transform Values with Table Calculations - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_tablecalculations.htm)

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about table calculations are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: They are computed after the query result is returned to Tableau
Their addressing and partitioning settings affect the result
They always run before context filters

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because table calculations operate on the result set in Tableau. **B** is correct because compute-using settings determine direction and restart behavior. **C** and **D** are incorrect. [Transform Values with Table Calculations - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_tablecalculations.htm)

questions.stem: A running total unexpectedly restarts for each Region pane. What setting should you check first?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because table calculation restart behavior depends on addressing and partitioning. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not control running-total restart boundaries. [Transform Values with Table Calculations - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_tablecalculations.htm)

questions.stem: Which Tableau function category is most appropriate for converting a string date into an actual date value for analysis?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because type conversion and date functions handle parsing and casting to dates. **B**, **C**, and **D** are not the relevant category. [Data Types - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/datafields_typesandroles_datatypes.htm)

questions.stem: Why are context filters sometimes used with TOP N filters?

options.text: To force Tableau to evaluate a prerequisite subset before later filters

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because context filters change the order of operations and can define the data subset before later filters such as Top N. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. [Tableau's Order of Operations - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/order_of_operations.htm)

questions.stem: Which TWO scenarios are good candidates for LOD expressions? (Select TWO)

options.text: Computing customer lifetime value independent of the current view grain
Finding percent difference from previous mark within a pane
Calculating a fixed denominator such as total sales per region

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because LODs set explicit granularity independent of the view. **C** is correct because LODs are useful for stable denominators. **B** is more naturally a table calculation, and **D** is layout only. [Level of Detail Expressions - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_calculatedfields_lod.htm)

questions.stem: A calculation using `WINDOW_SUM` returns unexpected values after a sort and pane change. What is the most likely reason?

options.text: The table calculation is sensitive to the view structure and compute-using settings
The data source must be republished
Tableau requires extracts for all window functions

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because window calculations depend on the visual partition and addressing in the current view. **B**, **C**, and **D** are false. [Transform Values with Table Calculations - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_tablecalculations.htm)

questions.stem: What is the purpose of Measure Names and Measure Values in Tableau?

options.text: To combine multiple measures in one view

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because Measure Names and Measure Values let multiple measures appear together in one worksheet. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/basicconnectoverview.htm

questions.stem: When building a dual-axis chart, what should you often do if the two axes represent the same unit?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because synchronized axes make comparisons meaningful when units are the same. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not solve the scaling issue. [Dashboards - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/dashboards.htm)

questions.stem: Which Tableau capability lets a user click a mark in one sheet and filter another sheet on the dashboard?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because filter actions pass selection context from one view to another. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not perform cross-sheet interaction. [Actions - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/actions.htm)

questions.stem: Which TWO conditions are important when creating a useful map in Tableau? (Select TWO)

options.text: Fields should have appropriate geographic roles
Latitude and longitude can be used for custom spatial positioning
Dashboards cannot include maps with actions

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because geographic roles help Tableau interpret location fields. **B** is correct because coordinates can drive custom maps. **C** and **D** are false. [Maps and Geographic Data Analysis in Tableau - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/maps.htm)
questions.reference: [Maps and Geographic Data Analysis in Tableau - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/maps.htm)

questions.stem: An analyst wants the percent of total to recalculate after users filter to a subset of products. Which approach is usually best?

options.text: Use a table calculation percent of total over the filtered marks
Convert the measure to a string

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because table-calculation percent-of-total can respond to the current filtered mark set. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not support interactive recalculation. [Transform Values with Table Calculations - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_tablecalculations.htm)

questions.stem: Which chart type is commonly used to show a distribution of a continuous measure across buckets?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because histograms show distributions across bins. **B**, **C**, and **D** are not the standard distribution view here. [Create Bins from a Continuous Measure - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_bins.htm)

questions.stem: Why might a highlight table be more appropriate than a plain text table?

options.text: It adds color encoding to help users spot magnitude patterns quickly

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because highlight tables pair tabular structure with color to reveal patterns. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. [Dashboards - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/dashboards.htm)

questions.stem: Which TWO dashboard design choices usually improve usability? (Select TWO)

options.text: Use actions and filters deliberately around a clear analytic flow
Keep important KPIs and filters visible without cluttering the canvas
Hide every legend and title by default
Mix unrelated metrics on one sheet to save space

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because thoughtful interaction supports exploration. **B** is correct because a clear layout improves comprehension. **C** and **D** often hurt usability. [Dashboards - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/dashboards.htm)

questions.stem: A scatter plot has millions of marks and is slow to render. Which change is most likely to help first?

options.text: Reduce mark density or aggregate the view before adding more detail
Duplicate the worksheet on the dashboard
Convert all dimensions to discrete dates

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because reducing mark volume or increasing aggregation often gives the biggest first performance improvement. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not directly reduce rendering workload. [Optimize Workbook Performance - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/performance_tips.htm)

questions.stem: What must a user generally have in order to overwrite an existing published workbook on Tableau Server or Tableau Cloud?

options.text: Appropriate publish/write permissions on the project or content
A local administrator account on their laptop
Ownership of the source database server

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because publishing and overwriting depend on Tableau permissions for the project/content. **B**, **C**, and **D** are not sufficient. [Publish a Workbook - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/publish_workbooks_howto.htm)

questions.stem: Why do embedded credentials matter for scheduled refreshes of published data sources?

options.text: They let the server authenticate to the underlying data source when the refresh runs
They remove the need for permissions

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because scheduled refreshes need valid stored credentials to reach the source system. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. [Publish a Workbook - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/publish_workbooks_howto.htm)

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about Tableau permissions are broadly correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: Permissions control capabilities such as viewing, web editing, and publishing
Project-level governance is important for consistent access management
Permissions are irrelevant once a workbook is downloaded
A dashboard action can replace content permissions

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because permissions determine allowed capabilities. **B** is correct because project governance helps apply access patterns consistently. **C** and **D** are false. [Permissions - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/server/en-us/permissions.htm)

questions.stem: A published extract refresh fails after the original publisher leaves the company. What is the most likely fix?

options.text: Update the saved credentials or ownership settings on the published data source

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because refresh failures after personnel changes usually relate to saved credentials or ownership/permission issues. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not address refresh authentication. [Publish a Workbook - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/publish_workbooks_howto.htm)

questions.stem: What is the main advantage of publishing a certified, shared data source for many analysts?

options.text: It promotes trusted reuse of common definitions and connections
It removes the need for permissions
It guarantees every workbook is fast

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because shared governed data sources improve consistency and reuse. **B**, **C**, and **D** are incorrect. [Publish a Workbook - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/publish_workbooks_howto.htm)

questions.stem: Which connection style is best when a dashboard must always query the freshest source data directly?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because live connections query the source system directly for current data. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/basicconnectoverview.htm

questions.stem: A parameter changes a threshold used in a calculated field, but nothing in the view changes until the calculation is used on the sheet. Why?

options.text: Parameters only affect the workbook through fields, filters, or analytics that reference them
Parameters are disabled for numeric values

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because parameters do nothing on their own; they must be referenced by some workbook logic. **B**, **C**, and **D** are false. [Transform Values with Table Calculations - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/calculations_tablecalculations.htm)

questions.stem: Which feature is most appropriate for guiding users through a sequence of narrative views in Tableau?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because story points present a sequenced narrative of views. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. [Dashboards - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/dashboards.htm)

questions.stem: A relationship-based model shows duplicated totals after the analyst switches to a physical join at the wrong grain. What is the root issue?

options.text: The join changed row cardinality and can multiply measures

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because physical joins can multiply rows when grains differ, which affects aggregated measures. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. [The Tableau Data Model - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/datasource_datamodel.htm)

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about Tableau order of operations are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: Context filters are evaluated before regular dimension filters
The order of operations can explain surprising interactions between LODs and filters
Color legends are always evaluated before extracts
Order of operations only matters on Tableau Server

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because context filters run earlier than regular dimension filters. **B** is correct because many filter/LOD interactions depend on operation order. **C** and **D** are false. [Tableau's Order of Operations - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/order_of_operations.htm)

questions.stem: A dashboard should let users click a state map and update a detailed sales table plus a trend chart. Which design is the most direct?

options.text: Use dashboard filter actions from the map to the target sheets
Convert the table to a story point

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because filter actions are designed for cross-sheet interactivity from selected marks. **B**, **C**, and **D** do not implement the requested behavior. [Actions - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/actions.htm)

questions.stem: Which area is most relevant when deciding whether a user may publish content to a Tableau project?
questions.explanation: **A** is correct because project permissions govern publishing capabilities. **B**, **C**, and **D** are unrelated. [Permissions - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/server/en-us/permissions.htm)

questions.stem: Why should dashboard designers usually avoid adding every possible filter and chart to one canvas?

options.text: Because visual clutter can hide the main analytic path and hurt performance
Because Tableau supports only four filters per dashboard
Because stories and dashboards cannot coexist

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because clutter makes dashboards harder to understand and can also hurt performance. **B**, **C**, and **D** are false. [Dashboards - Tableau](https://help.tableau.com/current/pro/desktop/en-us/dashboards.htm)
